Course Title: AI Triage Systems: Risk of Misclassification
Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Healthcare administrators, clinical managers, nurses, medical assistants, quality assurance staff, patient safety coordinators, healthcare IT staff, emergency department personnel
Team or Dept this is for: Clinical Operations, Patient Safety, Quality Assurance, Emergency Services, Healthcare IT, Risk Management, Medical Affairs
Description: When urgent cases are deprioritised or missed entirely.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with clinical workflows or healthcare settings, along with a general curiosity about how artificial intelligence is being used in patient care, is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key ways in which AI triage systems can misclassify urgent cases and explain the potential consequences for patient safety in healthcare settings. You will also be able to apply practical strategies to recognise warning signs of deprioritisation errors and support safer, more reliable triage processes within your organisation.
